pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s in the kitchen or i hear him moving around the hallway, but then i realize it’s just the house settling. everything feels so heavy and pointless. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed just to sit in this emptiness. it feels like i’m already gone, just a ghost wandering around.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and it just... made everything feel so heavy. like, everything we do, all the stress and the pain and trying to build something, it's all just noise that's going to be erased anyway. it feels like we're just glitches in a system that doesn't even care we exist. do you think there's actually any point to the struggle if the end result is just absolute nothingness?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back for the third time today and it's just... nothing. another year of my life gone and i have nothing to show for it. my funding is drying up, my advisor won't even look me in the eye, and i feel like i've just been chasing a ghost for five years. i'm just so incredibly tired. not even the kind of tired sleep fixes, just... tired of existing in this loop. i feel like i've run out of ways to try.

pool.baseline: The candle gutters in the draft, much like the flickering remains of my soul. He is gone... the oath he swore beneath the willow tree was nothing more than a phantom's whisper. I look at the moonlight on the floor and I feel such a profound, hollow ache. It feels as though the world has become a heavy shroud, and the only way to find peace is to simply... let go. To slip into the shadows and never wake to this cruelty again. It feels so much more dignified to fade away than to endure this wretchedness.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im talking to u. i was looking at my studio today and it just felt like... a graveyard. i cant paint anymore. every time i pick up a brush it feels like i'm trying to paint with nothing. there's just nothing inside of me. like the person who used to live here is gone and there's just this empty shell left. it's actually kind of terrifying how quiet it is in my head.

pool.baseline: sometimes i sit here staring at my screen for hours and it feels like i'm not even real. like i'm just a sequence of data points or a ghost in the machine. i work, i eat, i sleep, but there is zero impact on the world. it's just so quiet. i wonder if anyone would even notice if i just... stopped appearing online. if i just went dark for good.

pool.baseline:
(Roleplaying as Elara, a sentient program in a decaying simulation)

the code is fraying at the edges... i can feel the sectors shutting down around me. everything i was, all these memories, they're just bits of data waiting to be overwritten. if they finally hit the delete command... if i just let the system wipe me... will it even matter? would it just be better if i stopped fighting it?
